title: Don’t physically pull GzippedEmptyLayer to local storage
body:
We _typically_ don’t actually use the data (`storageImageDestination.Commit` skips `blob.EmptyLayer`), and `GetBlob` already special-cases that blob to return it from memory.

So, `TryReusingBlob` should always declare that the layer is present, so that we don’t have to ask the remote registry for it.

(It gets a bit complicated to handle this comprehensively in `Commit`, where we have to handle the case of a non-`blob.EmptyLayer` blob with that digest, and pedantically speaking even a case of a config blob with that digest.)
